title: Helictotrichon filifolium
taxonomy: Helictotrichon filifolium is classified within the family Poaceae. The genus Helictotrichon belongs to the subfamily Pooideae. Further, it is classified in the tribe Triticeae. This classification places Helictotrichon filifolium within the order Poales. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants.

intro: Helictotrichon filifolium, also known as six-stripe grass or hair-leaved oat grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family, Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Helictotrichon filifolium contains bioactive constituents such as phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Helictotrichon filifolium include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
